revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Jinne-in]][[ja:神恵院]]
{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Jinnein 02.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kan'onji|qid=Q861270|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.133986111111|N|133.64733333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}}}

{{infobox religious building|ja|日本の寺院
 | Name = {{ill|Jinne-in|ja|神恵院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinne-in|qq=}}
 | image = [[File:Zinnein 20201129 1.jpg|250px]]
 | image description = Daishidō (Main Hall)
 | Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|qid=Q861270|lt=Kanonji City|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}} Hachiman-chō 1-2-7
 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|8|2.35|N|133|38|50.4|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=神恵院}}
 | Mountain name (Sangō) = {{ill|Chilbosan|en|Chilbosan|qid=Q1207232|lt=Shippōzan|1=Chilbosan}}
 | Sect = {{ill|Shingon Daikakuji Sect|ja|真言宗大覚寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Daikakuji Sect|1=Shingon-shū Daikakuji-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10437754}}
 | Main image (Honzon) = {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}
 | Year of foundation = In the 3rd year of {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|qid=Q423370|lt=Daibō|1=Taiho}} (703)
 | Originator = {{ill|Nisshō|ja|日証上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nisshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
 | Official name = Shippōzan Jinne-in
 | Pilgrimage number = {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku 88 Temples|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} No. 68
 | Official website = [http://www.shikoku88-6869.com/jiin.html]
 | Official website name = 神恵院・観音寺
 | Map 2 = {{Switcher
    | {{Location map+|Japan Kagawa|width=230|float=center|places=
       {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|label=神恵院|lat_deg=34|lat_min=8|lat_sec=2.35|lon_deg=133|lon_min=38|lon_sec=50.4|mark=Indian_Swastika.svg|marksize=12|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=230}}
       {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|lat_deg=34|lat_min=21|lat_sec=2.46|lon_deg=134|lon_min=2|lon_sec=48.98|position=top|mark=blue pog.svg|label={{ill|Takamatsu Station|en|Takamatsu Station|qid=Q847367|lt=Kagawa Station|1=Takamatsu Station}}|label_size=95}}
       {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|lat_deg=34|lat_min=12|lat_sec=51|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=56|position=bottom|mark=BSicon FLUGg.svg|marksize=10|label={{ill|Takamatsu Airport|en|Takamatsu Airport|qid=Q1334603|lt=Takamatsu Airport|1=Takamatsu Airport}}|label_size=95}}
     }}
    | Caption = Location in Kagawa Prefecture
    | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=230}}
    | Caption2 = Location within Kanonji City
 }}
}}
[[File:Zinnein 0ɨ.jpg|220px|thumb|Nio statues at the Nio Gate]]
'''Jinne-in''' (神恵院) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Daikakuji Sect|ja|真言宗大覚寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Daikakuji Sect|1=Shingon-shū Daikakuji-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10437754}} located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|qid=Q861270|lt=Kanonji City|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}}. It is known by the mountain name {{ill|Chilbosan|en|Chilbosan|qid=Q1207232|lt=Shippōzan|1=Chilbosan}} and is temple number 68 on the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku 88 Temples|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} pilgrimage. Its principal image is {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}.
* (Additional traditional details about the honzon and recited verses are maintained in the original text.)

== History ==
According to tradition, the monk {{ill|Nisshō|ja|日証上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nisshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|East Asian Yogācāra|en|East Asian Yogācāra|qid=Q349849|lt=Hōsō Sect|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} was practicing on Mount Kotohibashi when he saw an elderly man playing a koto on a boat at sea. Realizing that the old man was {{ill|Hachiman Daimyōjin|ja|八幡大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q261637}}, he enshrined the koto and the boat on the mountain and named the shrine {{ill|Kotohiki Hachimangū|en|Kotohiki Hachimangū|qid=Q3199184|lt=Kotobiki Hachiman Shrine|1=Kotohiki Hachimangū}}. Later, after {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} visited in the 6th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} (722) and then in the 2nd year of {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|qid=Q295082|lt=Daidō|1=Datong (disambiguation)}} when {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} came and enshrined an image of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, the Kotobiki Hachiman Shrine was designated as a sacred site when the pilgrimage system was established. (At that time, the temple’s honorific name was Jinne-in; since the Meiji period the temple name has been fixed as Jinne-in.) However, due to the Meiji [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] edict, the image of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}} originally enshrined at Kotobiki Hachiman Shrine was transferred to the West Kindo (Sai-kondō) within the temple grounds, making that building the main hall of Jinne-in.
== Layout ==
{{gallery
|File:Zinneinɨɨ.jpg|180px|thumb|Heavenly maiden under the canopy
|File:Jinnein 01.JPG|Entrance to the Main Hall
|File:Zinnein 20250208 1.jpg|Main Hall
|File:Yosyuden 20201129 1.jpg|Yoshu-den
|File:Kanonzi01.jpg|Gigien Garden
}}
* Mountain Gate (shared with {{ill|Gwaneumsa|en|Gwaneumsa|qid=Q840756|lt=Kannon-ji|1=Gwaneumsa}})
* Geta Sound Tenzenshin Shrine: Located at the upper left of the approach from the mountain gate; according to legend, a visitor’s geta sound was heard but when pursued the figure vanished at this shrine.
* Main Hall: Features an “Amida Trinity Welcoming” display with a central standing statue of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, a standing statue of {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}} on the left, additional statues of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}} with sun and moon images on the left flank, and a standing statue of {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu|1=Kṣitigarbha}} on the right.
* Daishi Hall: Located on the right side of the Daishi Hall block; it houses an image of the Great Master.
* Prince Hall: On the left side of the Daishi Hall block; enshrines {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}.
* A strolling Japanese garden “Gigien” designed by the 45th Taisei Daisōjō Dōson.
* Yoshu-den: A hall enshrining Enma Daioh that also functions as a place for the recitation ceremony, reception, and temple administration; named after the “Yoshu Jūōkyō.”
When proceeding through the Nio Gate along the approach, a stone staircase leads upward; at its top, a bell tower is to the right, and a further set of stairs beyond a concrete structure leads to the Main Hall. The Daishi Hall is located to the right on the descent. Although the garden belongs to Kannon-ji, it is visible from the left side of Jinne-in’s Main Hall.
* Lodging: None
* Parking: Available free in front of the mountain gate.

== Cultural Properties ==
; Important Cultural Properties
* Silk-colored, hand-painted scroll of the original image of the Kotobiki Hachiman local Buddha: depicts the Amida Trinity on the upper left and the Shakyamuni Tathāgata Trinity on the lower right＜ref＞{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kanonji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/30/560.html|title=Silk-colored, Hand-painted Scroll of the Kotobiki Hachiman Local Buddha Image|publisher=Kanonji City|date=2016-03-22|accessdate=2020-03-15}}＜/ref＞. (Kamakura period work; designated on March 27, 1901 (Meiji 34))＜ref＞{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|1891|Silk-colored, Hand-painted Kotobiki Shrine Scroll}}＜/ref＞.
* (Further detailed items of enclosed materials are listed, including numerous volumes of inscriptions, wrapped items, coins, and other artifacts.)
These items were designated as Important Cultural Properties beginning in 1986 (Showa 61), with additions in 1988 (Showa 63) and 1989 (Heisei 1). In 2001 (Heisei 13) the designation name was updated. ＜ref＞(Official government notices: Showa 61, June 6; Showa 63, June 6; Heisei 1, June 12; Heisei 13, June 22)＜/ref＞.

== Nearby Sacred Sites ==
; {{ill|Shinbutsu Pilgrimage Route|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Pilgrimage Route|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10444949}}
: 104 {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|qid=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}}  –  '''105 Jinne-in'''  – 106 {{ill|Kannon-ji (Kanonji)|ja|観音寺 (観音寺市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji (Kanonji)|1=Kan'on-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630610}}

== Access ==
; Railway
* {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|qid=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|観音寺駅|en|観音寺駅|qid=Q11630636|lt=Kannon-ji Station (Kagawa)|1=観音寺駅}} (1.6km)
; Road
* General: {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture Road 21|ja|香川県道21号丸亀詫間豊浜線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture Road 21|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 21|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667500}} at Sankaibashi Kitazume (0.4km)
* Expressway: {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|en|Takamatsu Expressway|qid=Q7677132|lt=Takamatsu Expressway|1=Takamatsu Expressway}} at {{ill|Ōnohara IC|ja|大野原IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnohara IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大野原インターチェンジ|1=Onohara Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10938261}} (5.2km)

== Oku-no-in ==
; Kotobiki Hachiman Shrine (former sacred site No. 68)
* Location: {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|qid=Q861270|lt=Kanonji City|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}}, Hachiman-chō 1-1-1 ({{Coord|34|07|58|N|133|38|48|E|scall:100000|Kotobiki Hachiman Shrine|name=琴弾八幡宮}})
{{Main|{{ill|Kotohiki Hachimangū|en|Kotohiki Hachimangū|qid=Q3199184|lt=Kotobiki Hachiman Shrine|1=Kotohiki Hachimangū}}}}

== Adjacent Sacred Sites ==
; {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku 88 Temples|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}
: 67 {{ill|Daiko-ji|ja|大興寺 (Mitō City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiko-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} --{{small|(8.7km)}}-* '''68 Jinne-in''' --{{small|(0.1km)}}-- 69 {{ill|Kannon-ji (Kanonji)|ja|観音寺 (観音寺市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon-ji (Kanonji)|1=Kan'on-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630610}}

== Extra Nearby Sacred Sites ==
[[File:Ikikizizouson 01.jpg|180px|thumb|The Ikiki Jizō Statue]]
; Ikiki Jizō Statue
: In 1837 (Tenpō 7), Mori Yasurizaemon carved a five‐shaku (≈1.5 m) Jizō statue into a great camphor tree (≈30 m tall, 10 m trunk circumference, estimated age ca. 1,200 years) for his ailing daughter.
:* Location: {{ill|Kan'onji, Kagawa|en|Kan'onji, Kagawa|qid=Q861270|lt=Kanonji City|1=Kan'onji, Kagawa}}, Ōnohara-chō, Ōnohara 1208 (≈2 km from Ōnohara IC) ({{Coord|34|5|7.52|N|133|40|3.01|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ikiki Jizō Statue}})
